title: Ensure Modifiers are Declared in the Correct Order
description: The Java Language Specification recommends listing modifiers in a specific order to enhance code readability. Maintaining this order is a standard convention most developers are accustomed to.
recommendation: Adhere to the recommended order of modifiers as specified by the Java Language Specification.
impact: Non-adherence to this rule doesn't have any technical impact, but it reduces code readability.

noncompliant:
static public void main(String[] args) {   // Noncompliant
}
compliant:
public static void main(String[] args) {   // Compliant
}

checks.message: Modifiers should be declared in the correct order: public static final.
